attacking_style: Does not attack traditionally; opportunistic sprinter who capitalizes on late chaos when leadout duties end or sprinters crash. Wins come from final 200m sprint power, not positioning breakaways.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma deploys comprehensive flat-stage tempo control to protect jersey positions, even when no sprint interest exists. Functions primarily as leadout domestique until opportunity presents itself.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages ideal for primary role. Surprisingly capable on climbs at 60kph pace to create splits, showing versatility beyond pure sprinter profile. Cobbled classics present (limited success in Roubaix).
vulnerability: Relies heavily on chaos or crashes to access sprint opportunities when designated sprinter present. Early shutdown in cobbled classics (Roubaix trio neutralized). Not a primary sprinter by design.
key_patterns: Won yellow jersey through crash-related opportunity, not planned sprint setup (Tour 2019).
Team executes full-stage control for jersey defense even without sprint objective (Vuelta 2022).
Demonstrates mature race intelligence: skipping pulls to conserve energy for jersey defense (Tour 2019).
Versatile beyond sprint role: paced 60kph on climbs creating decisive splits (Paris-Nice 2022).
Consistently fills dual role: leadout domestique converting to opportunistic finisher when needed.
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 data shows evolution from pure leadout to climber-capable racer creating splits at 60kph, plus red jersey acquisition demonstrates continued opportunism
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2
